decisions.qaRationale: Our class is one specific facet (the A-G efficiency class label) of schema.org's broader EnergyConsumptionDetails, which encompasses energy-consumption information generally, so ours is the narrower concept.

decisions.bulkRationale: The schema.org property is a general upper bound for any characteristic, while our term specifically denotes the maximum score of a repair criterion.
decisions.qaRationale: Our term is a specialized maximum bounded to a repair criterion's score, whereas schema:maxValue is the generic upper value of any characteristic, so ours is strictly narrower than the upstream property.

decisions.bulkRationale: eudr:areaSize refers specifically to field area size, whereas schema.org/size is a general product dimension that can include many other measurements.
decisions.qaRationale: eudr:areaSize is a QuantitativeValue for a geographic field's area (hectares/ares/m²), a specific instance of schema.org/size's general product-dimension concept, so ours is the narrower property.

decisions.bulkRationale: Both properties record a date of a processing event, but transformationDate refers to commodity processing while productionDate refers to manufacturing or assembly.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are properties recording the date of a processing/production event on a product, but EUDR's transformation date is a regulation-specific commodity-processing concept that overlaps with but is not interchangeable with GS1's manufacturer production/assembly date.

decisions.bulkRationale: Both properties record a date related to the creation or processing of a product, but transformation refers specifically to processing/transformation while production denotes manufacturing.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are date-valued properties marking a creation/processing point, and overlap substantially, but transformation/processing of a commodity is not interchangeable with a manufacturer-determined production/assembly date-time, so they are close rather than exact.

decisions.bulkRationale: hasBatchLotNumber covers any batch or lot number for traceability, while exemptionScopeReference applies only to identifiers that are within a temporary exemption.
decisions.qaRationale: exemptionScopeReference can hold a batch/lot identifier scoped to an exemption, but it also covers serial identifiers and is semantically tied to exemption scope rather than general traceability, making it a narrower, specialized subset of hasBatchLotNumber only when batch-scoped.

decisions.bulkRationale: The FSC Certification property refers to a specific type of certification, whereas hasCertification is a generic property for any certification information.
decisions.qaRationale: Our FSC-specific certification property is a narrower specialization of schema.org's generic hasCertification, and both are properties relating a product to certification information.

decisions.bulkRationale: eudr:fscCertification refers specifically to FSC certification, whereas gs1:Has Certification is a generic property for any certification.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are properties linking a product to its certification; ours is restricted to FSC certifications specifically, making it a strict specialization of gs1's generic certification property.

decisions.bulkRationale: Both properties represent a list of geographic coordinates that define an area, though the data format differs.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are properties expressing geographic coordinates that delimit an area, but ours is a CBV-formatted polygon string scoped to plots >4ha while the upstream is a generic structured coordinate list for any geometry extent, so they overlap without being interchangeable.

decisions.bulkRationale: eudr:geofence specifies a polygon coordinate array for large plots, whereas locn#geometry is a general property linking any resource to its geometry.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are properties expressing the geometry of a location, and eudr:geofence is a constrained specialization (a coordinate-array polygon for plots >4ha) of locn:geometry's general resource-to-geometry association, so ours is narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: Both properties represent geographic coordinates, but schema.org’s geo is intended for places while our geolocation is specific to OriginDetails.
decisions.qaRationale: Both express geographic coordinates as a property, but schema.org's geo ranges over a GeoCoordinates/GeoShape object on a Place whereas eudr:geolocation carries a Geo URI string on OriginDetails, so they overlap without being interchangeable.

decisions.bulkRationale: eudr:geolocation is a single Geo URI for a plot, whereas m8g:coordinates represents a list of coordinates defining an extent.
decisions.qaRationale: eudr:geolocation is a single Geo URI point/coordinate for one small plot, a specific case of m8g:coordinates which is a list of coordinates defining a geometry's extent, but the structural mismatch (single anyURI vs coordinate list) keeps this short of CLOSE.

decisions.bulkRationale: eudr:exemptionEffectiveFrom specifies the start date of an exemption’s validity, a more specific instance of the general regulatory reference applicability start date.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are property-typed applicability start dates, but ours is the start of an exemption's validity (a narrower, exemption-scoped case) versus the upstream's general start date for any referenced regulatory information.

decisions.bulkRationale: eudr:countryList refers to a list of origin countries, whereas schema.org/addressCountry denotes a single country value; the former is a broader concept.
decisions.qaRationale: Both concern country-of-origin/location, but eudr:countryList is a list of countries on a Product while schema.org/addressCountry is a single country value within a PostalAddress/Place, so ours is the broader (multi-valued, product-scoped) concept.

decisions.bulkRationale: The term specifies the country where steel was melted and poured, which is a more specific instance of product origin covered by schema.org’s countryOfOrigin.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are properties; melt-and-pour country is a precise, regulation-specific (CBAM) form of product origin that falls under schema.org's deliberately broad, context-varying countryOfOrigin, making ours the narrower term.

decisions.bulkRationale: The schema.org property represents a general serial or alphanumeric identifier for any product, whereas the OUR term specifically denotes the manufacturer’s product/article number for a steel product.
decisions.qaRationale: schema.org serialNumber denotes a per-instance serial/alphanumeric product identifier, while our productNumber is a manufacturer's model/article number; these overlap as product identifiers but our term is the narrower, more specific notion so NARROW is acceptable though CLOSE is defensible.

decisions.bulkRationale: Heat Number is a specific type of batch/lot identifier for molten steel, making it narrower than the generic Batch/Lot Identifier.
decisions.qaRationale: Heat number is a steel-specific production-lot identifier and GS1's batch/lot definition explicitly enumerates production lot numbers, so ours is a genuine specialization, though the referent (molten-steel melt vs trade-item lot) is not a perfectly clean subsumption.

decisions.bulkRationale: The GS1 property is a generic batch/lot identifier for any trade item, while the OUR term specifies a delivery lot number for steel products.
decisions.qaRationale: Our term is a steel-specific delivery lot number constrained to IronSteelProduct, a specialization of GS1's general batch/lot identifier for any trade item, so it is narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: The OUR class is a specific certification for metallic products per EN 10204, while the upstream class represents any generic certification.
decisions.qaRationale: Our EN 10204 material test certificate is a specific kind of product certification, so it is narrower than GS1's generic Certification class, which legitimately covers product certifications.

decisions.bulkRationale: Material Test Certificate is a specific type of certification for metallic products, whereas Certification in schema.org is a general concept covering all kinds of certifications.
decisions.qaRationale: A material test certificate is an EN 10204 inspection/test document recording measured batch values, which overlaps with schema:Certification's attestation-of-characteristics intent but is not cleanly subsumed by it since types 2.1/2.2 are the manufacturer's own declarations rather than an independent certification body's authoritative credential.

decisions.bulkRationale: The OUR property identifies a casting run within a heat, whereas the upstream property refers to the heat itself, making it more general.
decisions.qaRationale: Cast number identifies a casting run within a heat, so it is a finer-grained subdivision of the upstream heat number, making OUR term narrower than the heat identifier.

decisions.bulkRationale: The cast number is a specific type of batch/lot identifier used for traceability of a casting run, whereas the GS1 property is a generic batch/lot identifier.
decisions.qaRationale: A cast number is a steel-specific production-lot identifier that falls within GS1's explicitly broad batch/lot concept (which names 'production lot number' among its examples), so our term is a narrower specialization of the same property.

decisions.bulkRationale: Schema.org size is a general property for any product dimension, while the OUR term specifies a numeric nominal size of a tested material.
decisions.qaRationale: Our term is a specific numeric nominal dimension (mm) of a steel test product, which is one constrained sub-case of schema.org's general product size property, so ours is narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: Garment Type is a specific garment category, while schema.org’s category is a general classification that can encompass many item types.
decisions.qaRationale: garmentType is a specialized garment-classification slot that is subsumed by schema.org's general-purpose category property, so ours is narrower than the target.

decisions.bulkRationale: A dyeing facility is a specific type of manufacturing plant, making it more specialized than the general Has Manufacturing Plant property.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are product-to-facility properties and dyeing/printing is a manufacturing step, so eutex:dyeingFacility is a role-specialized subproperty of the general gs1:manufacturingPlant relation.

decisions.bulkRationale: Both refer to a monetary reduction, but the take‑back incentive is specifically tied to product returns, whereas discount applies generally to any order.
decisions.qaRationale: Both denote a monetary reduction, but takeBackIncentive is a string description scoped to product-return programs while schema:discount is a general order-level discount, making ours the narrower concept.

decisions.bulkRationale: The GS1 ‘Size’ property refers to a general linkage to size groupings and dimensions, whereas our ‘sizeRange’ provides a specific string of available sizes.
decisions.qaRationale: Our sizeRange is a single xsd:string property listing the available size range, whereas gs1:size links a product to structured gs1:SizeDetails groupings, so ours expresses a narrower, simpler facet of the same size concept.

decisions.bulkRationale: eutex:TestStandard denotes a specific type of textile durability test standard, while UNTP Standard is a generic concept for any standard; the former is a narrower subclass.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are classes; eutex:TestStandard is a domain-specific subtype (textile durability/robustness test standards) of the generic untp:Standard concept, so ours is narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: Both properties specify the method used for a measurement, but our property is specific to spirality tests whereas the candidate is a general measurement method.
decisions.qaRationale: Our property is a domain-specific specialization (spirality test method reference) of the general schema:measurementMethod that names the method used for a measurement, so it is narrower rather than interchangeable.

decisions.bulkRationale: Weaving Facility refers specifically to a location where yarn is woven or knitted, which is a subset of the broader Manufacturing Plant concept.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are product-to-facility properties and a weaving/knitting facility is a specific type of manufacturing plant, so our term is a narrower specialization of gs1:manufacturingPlant.

decisions.bulkRationale: Textile Footwear is a specific type of footwear, making it narrower than the general Footwear class.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are footwear classes, but our term restricts to textile-containing footwear, a proper subset of gs1:Footwear, so ours is narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: Textile Footwear is a specific type of Wearable Product, making it a narrower subclass.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are classes and textile footwear is a strict subset of products worn on the body, so our term is narrower than gs1:WearableProduct.

decisions.bulkRationale: Our property specifies third‑party certification for recycled content claims, whereas hasCertification is a general certification property applicable to any product or entity.
decisions.qaRationale: Our property is a specialized third-party certification of recycled-content claims on a RecycledContentDeclaration, a strict specialization of schema:hasCertification's general product/organization certification, so ours is narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: Our property is a specific third‑party certification for recycled content claims, whereas the GS1 property is a general certification information field.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are properties sharing the gs1:CertificationDetails range, but ours is gs1:certification scoped specifically to third-party verification of recycled-content claims, making it a narrower specialization.

decisions.bulkRationale: Both terms refer to the percentage of organic content in a product, though the upstream definition is missing.
decisions.qaRationale: Identical name and property type suggest the same concept family, but the upstream term carries no definition while ours is narrowly scoped to certified organic fibre by mass under EU 2018/848, so identity cannot be confirmed and only an overlapping match is defensible.

decisions.bulkRationale: Both express a weight‑based percentage of material, but the upstream term covers any textile material while ours specifically refers to secondary (recycled) material.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are weight-based material percentages, but ours is restricted to secondary/recycled material while the GS1 term covers any textile material component, making ours a narrower specialization.

decisions.bulkRationale: Our property specifies a visual inspection test method, which is a specific type of measurement technique covered by schema.org’s generic measurementMethod.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are properties naming the method used; our visual-inspection test-method reference is a domain-specific specialization of schema.org's generic measurementMethod, hence narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: The GS1 sustainabilityInfo link covers all sustainability and recycling information, while the takeBackUrl specifically points to take‑back program details.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are link-type properties and a take-back program URL is a specific subset of GS1's general sustainability-and-recycling information link, so OUR term is narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: The candidate property covers any additional product classification, while the apparelSubcategory is a specific sub‑classification within that broader category.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are classification properties on a product and apparelSubcategory is a specific kind of additional product classification, so ours is the narrower term, though the fit is approximate since GS1's property targets generic classification-detail objects rather than a controlled apparel taxonomy.

decisions.bulkRationale: schema.org category is a general item classification, whereas eutex:apparelSubcategory specifies a particular apparel subcategory.
decisions.qaRationale: schema:category is a generic item-classification property whose value space subsumes apparel subcategories, so our EU-specific apparel subcategory property is a narrower specialization of it.

decisions.bulkRationale: robustnessAssessment refers to a general assessment of textile robustness, whereas robustnessScore is a specific numeric score.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are product-level properties about textile robustness, but ours yields a full RobustnessAssessment object that would encompass the single numeric robustnessScore the upstream property captures, making ours broader.

decisions.bulkRationale: Both describe a numeric evaluation, but the visual inspection result is a specific type of rating defined by ISO 15487.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are classes representing a numeric-scale evaluation, and the ISO 15487 visual inspection result is a domain-specific specialization of the generic schema.org Rating.

decisions.bulkRationale: Both properties represent a numeric rating value, but schema.org’s ratingValue is generic while recyclabilityScore is specifically for recyclability assessment.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are numeric rating properties, but schema.org's ratingValue is a generic rating tied to schema:Rating while eutex:recyclabilityScore is a domain-specific composite recyclability metric on RecyclabilityAssessment, so they overlap without being interchangeable.

decisions.bulkRationale: The cut and sew facility is a specific type of manufacturing plant, so it is narrower than the general Has Manufacturing Plant property.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are product-to-facility properties and a cut-and-sew (garment assembly) site is a specific kind of manufacturing plant, so our term is a narrower specialization of gs1:manufacturingPlant.

decisions.bulkRationale: The upstream property denotes a general location of an organization, while ours specifies the particular facility where garment cutting and sewing occur.
decisions.qaRationale: Our cut-and-sew facility property is a specialized subtype of the upstream general organization-location property, designating one specific production-step location.

decisions.bulkRationale: RecyclabilityAssessment is a specific assessment, whereas CircularityPerformance is a broader concept covering overall circularity.
decisions.qaRationale: Recyclability is one dimension of circularity, so our recyclability-specific scoring class falls under the broader CircularityPerformance class, though the upstream term's missing definition leaves some uncertainty.

decisions.bulkRationale: Robustness Assessment is a specific numeric rating for cleaning durability, whereas Rating denotes any generic numeric evaluation.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are classes and our composite 0-10 robustness score is a domain-specific instance of a numeric evaluation on a scale, making it a narrower specialization of schema.org Rating.

decisions.bulkRationale: Recycled Content Source specifies the origin of recycled material, whereas recycledContent refers to the recycled material itself.
decisions.qaRationale: recycledContentSource names the origin/feedstock of recycled material while oec:recycledContent denotes the recycled content itself, so ours is a narrower facet of that broader concept.

decisions.bulkRationale: Textile Apparel is a specific type of Wearable Product, making it a narrower subclass.
decisions.qaRationale: Textile Apparel (clothing) is a proper subset of gs1:WearableProduct, which also covers non-apparel worn items like footwear, eyewear, and jewelry, so our class-to-class match is genuinely narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: Our class represents a specific type of clothing (textile apparel), which is a narrower subclass of the general Clothing class.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are classes; textile apparel is a specific subset of clothing-worn-on-the-body (excluding non-textile clothing), so our class is properly narrower than gs1:Clothing.

decisions.bulkRationale: Our property specifies product weight excluding trims, a more specific subset of the general weight concept in schema.org.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are weight properties and our term is a specialized weight metric (excluding trims) that is a more specific subset of schema.org's generic product weight.

decisions.bulkRationale: The upstream property refers to a general link to instructions, while our term specifies a repair/care guide URL, making it a narrower specialization.
decisions.qaRationale: GS1 instructions is a general instructions link (assembly, usage, etc.) and our repair/care guide URL is a specific subtype of that link, so ours is narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: The candidate property describes a general manufacturing plant, whereas our property refers specifically to the facility where finishing treatments were applied.
decisions.qaRationale: A finishing facility is a specialized type of manufacturing plant (one performing the finishing sub-process), so our property is a narrower specialization of the broader gs1:manufacturingPlant relation, though role ambiguity about which facility is referenced keeps confidence moderate.

decisions.bulkRationale: The spinning facility is a specific type of manufacturing plant, making it a narrower concept.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are properties linking a product to a production facility, and a spinning facility is a specific kind of manufacturing plant, so our term is a genuine narrowing of gs1:manufacturingPlant.

decisions.bulkRationale: The candidate class is a generic category code, while our class specifies LCIA category codes per PEF methodology.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are classes representing category codes; ours is a domain-specific specialization restricted to PEF LCIA impact-assessment categories, making it strictly narrower than schema:CategoryCode's generic any-categorization-system code.

decisions.bulkRationale: Both properties represent the European Community (EINECS/ELINCS) identifier for a substance.
decisions.qaRationale: Both name the same EINECS/ELINCS European Community number identifier property; the textile term simply scopes the core oec:ecNumber to SubstanceOfConcern without changing the identifier's meaning, so they are interchangeable.

decisions.bulkRationale: The EC Number is a specific type of identifier, whereas schema.org/identifier represents any kind of identifier.
decisions.qaRationale: eutex:ecNumber is a specific kind of textual identifier (EINECS/ELINCS) and schema.org/identifier is the generic identifier supertype, so ours is strictly narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: The upstream property is a general certification indicator, while the OUR property specifies fiber‑specific certifications.
decisions.qaRationale: Our fiber-specific certification property is a specialization of schema.org's general product/organization certification property, so ours is narrower.

decisions.bulkRationale: The upstream property is a general certification indicator for products, whereas the OUR property applies only to fiber‑specific certifications.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are properties expressing a certification relation, but ours restricts the domain to textile fiber material and scopes to fiber-specific certifications, making it a specialization of the upstream general certification property.

decisions.bulkRationale: The candidate property refers to general service or maintenance instructions, whereas the OUR term specifically denotes repair services provided by an organization.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are properties about post-sale service, but ours covers only repair services while gs1:serviceInfo links to general service or maintenance instructions, making ours narrower in scope.

decisions.bulkRationale: The upstream property refers only to total carbon footprint, while our property covers the entire environmental footprint.
decisions.qaRationale: Environmental footprint conceptually subsumes carbon footprint as one of several impact indicators, so ours is broader, though the structured-declaration range versus a single total-value differs enough to keep confidence modest.

decisions.bulkRationale: The candidate class is a general recyclability assessment, while our term specifies technical recyclability based on technology.
decisions.qaRationale: Our term is a specific technical-recyclability assessment scoped to applicable recycling technology, making it a narrower kind of the upstream general RecyclabilityAssessment class.

decisions.bulkRationale: The GS1 property gives a generic percentage for any textile material, whereas our term specifies the percentage of elastane/spandex only.
decisions.qaRationale: Both are decimal material-percentage properties, but ours fixes the material to elastane (a specific case of GS1's generic per-material percentage), making ours a genuine specialization.
